subject: Biology
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: Assess strength of graft and plant vigor.
topics: xi) Ask groups to compare each other’s grafted plants during the time frame.
 If you were a farmer, which crop would you propagate by grafting and why?
 Grafting is an artificial method of asexual reproduction where tissues of two plants are joined so they grow as one.
activities: i) Put learners into small groups of 4-5
ii) Make all the necessary requirements available for the groups to use.
iii) Ask the groups to select a healthy rootstock and a bud from the scion plant.
iv) Ask groups to make a T-shaped cut on the bark of the rootstock.
v) Ask the groups to carefully insert the bud into the cut, ensuring cambium contact.
vi) Ask the groups to wrap the union firmly with budding tape or polythene strip, leaving the bud exposed.
